message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Activate test: "should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 2 live neighbors" (line 8)
Current state: 3 tests passing
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.

content.text: Let me start by reading the current state of the test and implementation files.

Good. I can see the current state. Three tests are passing (lines 5-13), and line 14 has the `it.todo()` I need to activate. The implementation currently returns an empty array `[]`.

### Step 1: Activate the Test

I'll convert line 14 from `it.todo()` to an executable test. For "should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 2 live neighbors", I need a grid where a live cell has exactly 2 live neighbors. A simple arrangement: a row of 3 cells `[true, true, true]` — the middle cell has 2 live neighbors (left and right).

input.old_string: it.todo("should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 2 live neighbors");

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-52-50_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/game-of-life.spec.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text: Let me verify the file looks correct.

The test file looks correct. One test activated, all others remain as `it.todo()`.

### Step 2: Prediction - Compilation Error

The `nextGeneration` function already exists in the implementation file, so there will be no compilation error. The function is already imported and the signature matches. This means we skip directly to a runtime error on first run.

Since the function already exists (the implementation has `export function nextGeneration(grid: boolean[][]): boolean[][]`), there is no compilation error to predict. We go straight to a runtime assertion error.

### Step 3: Run Test - Verify (No Compilation Error Expected)

```
Red Phase - Compilation Error Prediction:
- Test: "should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 2 live neighbors"
- Expected: No compilation error (function already exists)
- Reason: nextGeneration is already exported from game-of-life.ts
- Error: None expected
```
